original_abstract:
This study reports the synthesis, characterization, and theoretical investigation of a novel coumarin-triazole-thiophene hybrid: 4-(((4-ethyl-5-(thiophen-2-yl)-4H-1,2,4-triazol-3-yl)thio)methyl)-6,7-dimethyl-2H-chromen-2-one (1). The compound was synthesized via the reaction of 4-ethyl-5-(thiophen-2-yl)-4H-1,2,4-triazole-3-thiol and 4-(chloromethyl)-6,7-dimethyl-2H-chromen-2-one, with its structural identity confirmed through microanalysis, IR, and both 1H and 13C APT NMR spectroscopy. Density Functional Theory (DFT) calculations were employed to analyze the gas-phase molecular geometry and electronic properties, while reactivity descriptors and molecular electrostatic potential (MEP) mapping successfully identified the molecule’s primary reactive centers. 

The pharmacological profile was further assessed through ADMET modeling, which indicated favorable gastrointestinal absorption and a lack of blood-brain barrier penetration. Toxicity modeling classified the hybrid as a Class 4 substance. To evaluate its therapeutic potential against COVID-19, molecular docking simulations were conducted against several SARS-CoV-2 proteins. The results demonstrate that the title compound exhibits significant binding affinity across all tested targets, with the most efficient interaction observed against the Papain-like protease (PLpro). These binding dynamics were further validated and refined using molecular dynamics (MD) simulations.
humanized_abstract:
Title Synthesis, Characterization and Theoretical Study of Coumarin-Triazole-Thiophene Hybrid A new coumarin-triazole-thiophene hybrid, 4-(((4-ethyl-5-(thiophen-2-yl)-4H-1,2,4-triazol-3-yl)thio)methyl)-6,7-dimethyl-2H-chromen-2-one (1), was synthesized from reaction of 4-ethyl-5-(thiophen-2-yl)-4H-1,2,4-triazole-3-thiol and 4-(chloromethyl)-6,7-dimethyl-2H-chromen-2-one by 89% using spectral methods (microanalysis, IR, 1H and 13C APT NMR). The Density Functional Theory (DFT) method, at B3LYP level, was used for optimization of the ground state geometry of the compound and the evaluation of energy components. The reactivity indexes and molecular electrostatic potential (MEP) which give the locations of the reactive sites were calculated in the gas phase to elucidating the possible reactivity sites in the title compound.

Further investigation of the pharmacological profile revealed favorable ADMET properties (i.e., good absorption in GI tract, poor blood-brain barrier penetration and classified as Class 4 toxicity) and the hybrid interactions with SARS-CoV-2 associated proteins were confirmed by molecular docking simulations. The molecule exhibited affinity to all the targeted protein receptors, especially displaying high association with the Papain-like protease (PLpro) receptor. Furthermore, the docked pose binding interactions between the hybrid and the target protein (PLpro) were refined and validated with a molecular dynamics (MD) simulations study.

document.output:
Title Synthesis, Characterization and Theoretical Study of Coumarin-Triazole-Thiophene Hybrid A new coumarin-triazole-thiophene hybrid, 4-(((4-ethyl-5-(thiophen-2-yl)-4H-1,2,4-triazol-3-yl)thio)methyl)-6,7-dimethyl-2H-chromen-2-one (1), was synthesized from reaction of 4-ethyl-5-(thiophen-2-yl)-4H-1,2,4-triazole-3-thiol and 4-(chloromethyl)-6,7-dimethyl-2H-chromen-2-one by 89% using spectral methods (microanalysis, IR, 1H and 13C APT NMR). The Density Functional Theory (DFT) method, at B3LYP level, was used for optimization of the ground state geometry of the compound and the evaluation of energy components. The reactivity indexes and molecular electrostatic potential (MEP) which give the locations of the reactive sites were calculated in the gas phase to elucidating the possible reactivity sites in the title compound.

Further investigation of the pharmacological profile revealed favorable ADMET properties (i.e., good absorption in GI tract, poor blood-brain barrier penetration and classified as Class 4 toxicity) and the hybrid interactions with SARS-CoV-2 associated proteins were confirmed by molecular docking simulations. The molecule exhibited affinity to all the targeted protein receptors, especially displaying high association with the Papain-like protease (PLpro) receptor. document.input:
This study reports the synthesis, characterization, and theoretical investigation of a novel coumarin-triazole-thiophene hybrid: 4-(((4-ethyl-5-(thiophen-2-yl)-4H-1,2,4-triazol-3-yl)thio)methyl)-6,7-dimethyl-2H-chromen-2-one (1).
